title: Landing Page Optimisation
description: Design, build, and optimise a high-converting landing page. You'll learn the principles of conversion-focused design and test different approaches.
whyItMatters: A landing page is where marketing meets action. You can have the best ads in the world, but if the page they land on doesn't convert, you're wasting money. Understanding landing page optimisation is essential for every digital marketer.
prerequisites: Basic familiarity with web design tools

steps.title: Define the page goal
steps.description: What single action should visitors take? Sign up, buy, download, book a call? One page, one goal. Choose a product/service and target audience.

steps.title: Write the copy first
steps.description: Before any design, write: headline (attention), subheadline (clarity), benefits (3-5 bullet points), social proof (testimonials), and CTA (action). Follow the AIDA framework.
steps.tips: The headline is responsible for 80% of whether someone stays or leaves.

steps.title: Design the page
steps.description: Use Webflow, Unbounce, or Canva to create the page. Follow landing page best practices: hero section, benefits, social proof, CTA above and below the fold, minimal navigation.

steps.title: Optimise for mobile
steps.description: Over 60% of traffic is mobile. Ensure the page looks great and loads fast on phones. Test on your own device.

steps.title: Set up tracking
steps.description: Add Google Analytics or equivalent. Set up a conversion event for your CTA. Know your baseline metrics.

steps.title: Create a variant
steps.description: Design one alternative version (different headline, CTA colour, or layout). Document your hypothesis for what will perform better and why.

steps.title: Write a test plan
steps.description: Describe how you'd A/B test these variants: sample size needed, duration, success metric, and statistical significance threshold.

deliverables: Live or mockup landing page (version A)
Alternative version B with documented hypothesis
Copy document with headline, benefits, social proof, and CTA

rubric.description: The page has one clear goal and every element supports that goal.
Headlines are compelling, benefits are clear, and CTAs are action-oriented.
The page looks professional, loads fast, and works on mobile.
The A/B test plan is methodologically sound with a clear hypothesis.
